 {"id":"bd-03r","title":"Document deletions manifest in AGENTS.md and README","description":"Parent: bd-imj

## Task
Add documentation about the deletions manifest feature.

## Locations to Update

### AGENTS.md
- Explain that deletions.jsonl is tracked in git
- Document that `bd delete` records to the manifest
- Explain cross-clone propagation mechanism

### README.md  
- Brief mention in .beads directory structure section
- Link to detailed docs if needed

### docs/deletions.md (new file)
- Full technical documentation
- Format specification
- Pruning policy
- Git history fallback
- Troubleshooting

## Acceptance Criteria
- AGENTS.md updated with deletion workflow
- README.md mentions deletions.jsonl purpose
- New docs/deletions.md with complete reference","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-25T14:56:49.13027-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T15:17:23.145944-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T15:17:23.145944-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-0b2","title":"Need --no-git-history flag to disable git history backfill during import","description":"During JSONL migration (beads.jsonl → issues.jsonl), the git history backfill mechanism causes data loss by finding issues in the old beads.jsonl git history and incorrectly treating them as deleted.

A --no-git-history flag for 'bd import' and 'bd sync' would allow users to disable the git history fallback when it's causing problems.

Use cases:
- JSONL filename migrations
- Repos with complex git history
- Debugging import issues
- Performance (skip slow git scans)

Related: bd-0gh (migration causes spurious deletions)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-26T22:28:22.5286-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-26T23:10:49.354436-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-26T23:10:49.354436-08:00"}
+{"id":"bd-0io","title":"Sync should cleanup snapshot files after completion","description":"## Problem
`bd sync` leaves orphaned merge artifact files (beads.base.jsonl, beads.left.jsonl) after completion, causing:
1. Doctor warnings about 'Multiple JSONL files found'
2. Confusion during debugging
3. Potential stale data issues on next sync

## Root Cause
`SnapshotManager` creates these files for 3-way merge deletion tracking but `Cleanup()` is never called after sync completes (success or failure).

## Fix
Call `SnapshotManager.Cleanup()` at end of successful sync:

```go
// sync.go after successful validation
sm := NewSnapshotManager(jsonlPath)
sm.Cleanup()
```

## Files
- cmd/bd/sync.go (add cleanup call)
- cmd/bd/snapshot_manager.go (Cleanup method exists at line 188)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-28T17:06:20.881183-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-28T17:06:20.881183-08:00"}

+{"id":"bd-ov1","title":"Doctor: exclude merge artifacts from 'multiple JSONL' warning","description":"## Problem
`bd doctor` warns about 'Multiple JSONL files found' when merge artifact files exist:
```
JSONL Files: Multiple JSONL files found: beads.base.jsonl, beads.left.jsonl, issues.jsonl ⚠
```

This is confusing because these aren't real issue JSONL files - they're temporary snapshots for deletion tracking.

## Fix
Exclude known merge artifact patterns from the multiple-JSONL warning:

```go
// In doctor JSONL check
skipPatterns := map[string]bool{
    "beads.base.jsonl": true,
    "beads.left.jsonl": true, 
    "beads.right.jsonl": true,
}
```

## Files
- cmd/bd/doctor/ (JSONL check logic)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-28T17:06:26.266097-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-28T17:06:26.266097-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-qsm","title":"Auto-compact deletions during bd sync","description":"Parent: bd-imj

## Task
Optionally prune deletions manifest during sync when threshold exceeded.

**Note: Opt-in feature** - disabled by default to avoid sync latency.

## Implementation

In `bd sync`:
```go
func (s *Syncer) Sync() error {
    // ... existing sync logic ...
    
    // Auto-compact only if enabled
    if s.config.GetBool("deletions.auto_compact", false) {
        deletionCount := deletions.Count(".beads/deletions.jsonl")
        threshold := s.config.GetInt("deletions.auto_compact_threshold", 1000)
        
        if deletionCount > threshold {
            retentionDays := s.config.GetInt("deletions.retention_days", 7)
            if err := s.compactor.PruneDeletions(retentionDays); err != nil {
                log.Warnf("Failed to auto-compact deletions: %v", err)
                // Non-fatal, continue sync
            }
        }
    }
    
    // ... rest of sync ...
}
```

## Configuration
```yaml
deletions:
  retention_days: 7
  auto_compact: false              # Opt-in, disabled by default
  auto_compact_threshold: 1000     # Trigger when > N entries (if enabled)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Auto-compact disabled by default
- [ ] Enabled via config `deletions.auto_compact: true`
- [ ] Sync checks deletion count only when enabled
- [ ] Auto-prunes when threshold exceeded
- [ ] Failure is non-fatal (logged warning)
- [ ] Test: no compaction when disabled
- [ ] Test: compaction triggers when enabled and threshold exceeded","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-25T09:57:04.522795-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T15:03:01.469629-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T15:03:01.469629-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-tqo","title":"deletions.jsonl gets corrupted with full issue objects instead of deletion records","description":"## Bug Description

The deletions.jsonl file was found to contain full issue objects (like issues.jsonl) instead of deletion records.

### Expected Format (DeletionRecord)
```json
{"id":"bd-xxx","timestamp":"2025-...","actor":"user","reason":"deleted"}
```

### Actual Content Found
```json
{"id":"bd-03r","title":"Document deletions manifest...","description":"...","status":"closed",...}
```

## Impact
- bd sync sanitization step reads deletions.jsonl and removes any matching IDs from issues.jsonl
- With 60 full issue objects in deletions.jsonl, ALL 60 issues were incorrectly removed during sync
- This caused complete data loss of the issue database

## Root Cause (suspected)
Something wrote issues.jsonl content to deletions.jsonl. Possible causes:
- Export writing to wrong file
- File path confusion during sync
- Race condition between export and deletion tracking

## Related Issues
- bd-0b2: --no-git-history flag (just fixed)
- bd-4pv: export outputs only 1 issue after corruption  
- bd-4t7: auto-import runs during --no-auto-import

## Reproduction
Unknown - discovered during bd sync session on 2025-11-26

## Fix
Need to investigate what code path could write issue objects to deletions.jsonl","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-26T23:17:01.938931-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-26T23:25:21.445143-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-26T23:25:02.209911-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-tru","title":"Update documentation for bd prime and Claude integration","description":"Update AGENTS.md, README.md, and QUICKSTART.md to document the new `bd prime` command, `bd setup claude` command, and tip system.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-11T23:30:22.77349-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T17:47:30.807069-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T17:47:30.807069-08:00"}
+{"id":"bd-tt0","title":"Sync validation false positive: legitimate deletions trigger 'data loss detected'","description":"## Problem
`bd sync` fails with false positive data loss detection when legitimate deletions occur:
```
Post-import validation failed: import reduced issue count: 26 → 25 (data loss detected!)
```

## Root Cause
The validation in `sync.go:329-340` counts DB issues BEFORE import, but `purgeDeletedIssues()` in `importer.go:159` legitimately removes issues DURING import. The validation doesn't account for expected deletions.

**The Flow:**
```
sync.go:293     → beforeCount = countDBIssues() = 26
sync.go:310-319 → sanitizeJSONL removes deleted issues from JSONL (RemovedCount=N)
sync.go:323     → importFromJSONL() runs subprocess
                  └→ importer.go:159 purgeDeletedIssues() removes issues from DB
sync.go:331     → afterCount = countDBIssues() = 25
sync.go:335     → validatePostImport(26, 25) → ERROR
```

## Fix
Pass `sanitizeResult.RemovedCount` to validation and account for expected deletions:

```go
// sync.go around line 335
expectedDecrease := 0
if sanitizeResult != nil {
    expectedDecrease = sanitizeResult.RemovedCount
}
if err := validatePostImportWithDeletions(beforeCount, afterCount, expectedDecrease); err != nil {
    // ...
}
```

```go
// integrity.go - new or modified function
func validatePostImportWithDeletions(before, after, expectedDeletions int) error {
    if after < before - expectedDeletions {
        return fmt.Errorf("unexpected data loss: %d → %d (expected max decrease: %d)", 
            before, after, expectedDeletions)
    }
    // ...
}
```

## Files
- cmd/bd/sync.go:329-340
- cmd/bd/integrity.go:289-301","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-28T17:06:15.515768-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-28T17:06:15.515768-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-tys","title":"Git history fallback has incorrect logic for detecting deletions","description":"## Problem

The `wasInGitHistory` function in `internal/importer/importer.go:891` returns true if the ID is found **anywhere** in git history. But finding an ID in history doesn't necessarily mean it was deleted - it could mean:

1. The issue was added (appears in a commit adding it)
2. The issue was modified (appears in commits updating it)
3. The issue was deleted (appears in a commit removing it)

The current logic incorrectly treats all three cases as 'deleted'.

## Correct Logic

`git log -S` with `--oneline` shows commits where the string was added OR removed. To detect deletion specifically:

1. ID appears in git history (was once in JSONL)
2. ID is NOT currently in JSONL

The second condition is already checked by the caller (`purgeDeletedIssues`), so technically the logic is correct in context. But the function name and doc comment are misleading.

## Fix Options

1. **Rename function** to `wasEverInJSONL` and update doc comment to clarify
2. **Add explicit check** for current JSONL state in the function itself

Option 1 is simpler and correct since caller already filters.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-25T12:46:16.073661-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T15:11:54.426093-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T15:11:54.426093-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-v0x","title":"Auto-detect issue prefix from existing JSONL in 'bd init'","description":"When running `bd init` in a fresh clone with existing JSONL, it should auto-detect the issue prefix from the JSONL file instead of requiring `--prefix`.

Currently you must specify `--prefix ef` manually. But the JSONL file already contains issues like `ef-1it`, `ef-1jp` etc., so the prefix is known.

**Ideal UX**:
```
$ bd init
Detected issue prefix 'ef' from existing JSONL (38 issues).
✓ Database initialized...
```

This would make fresh clone hydration a single command: `bd init` with no flags.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-27T20:21:21.049215-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-27T20:21:21.049215-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-v29","title":"Deletions pruning doesn't include results in JSON output","description":"## Problem

When `bd compact --json` runs with deletions pruning, the prune results are silently discarded:

```go
// Only report if there were deletions to prune
if result.PrunedCount > 0 {
    if jsonOutput {
        // JSON output will be included in the main response
        return  // <-- BUG: results are NOT included anywhere
    }
    ...
}
```

## Location
`cmd/bd/compact.go:925-929`

## Impact
- JSON consumers don't know deletions were pruned
- No way to audit pruning via automation

## Fix
Return prune results and include in JSON output structure:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "compacted": {...},
  "deletions_pruned": {
    "count": 5,
    "retention_days": 7
  }
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-25T12:48:59.730979-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T15:11:54.363653-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T15:11:54.363653-08:00"}
